prose:
**Ferrocene Fukui Reactivity Analysis** — GFN2-xTB (xtb, semi-empirical tight-binding), gas phase, charge 0 / multiplicity 1 (with cation +1/doublet and anion −1/doublet computed to derive the Fukui indices). Geometry: input structure from `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/ferrocene/ferrocene.xyz` (21 atoms, not re-optimized). Fukui indices computed via finite differences in partial atomic charges (Mulliken, GFN2-xTB scheme) between neutral, singly oxidized (cation), and singly reduced (anion) states. Both charged states converged; charge conservation checks passed (Σf⁺ = 1.0000, Σf⁻ = 1.0000). **Key finding:** The iron center is the most electrophilic (f⁺ = 0.22885) and most nucleophilic (f⁻ = 0.25938) site in ferrocene, reflecting its central role in the molecule's electronic structure and reactivity. The dual descriptor (f⁺ − f⁻ = −0.0305) indicates slight nucleophilic preference. Cyclopentadienyl carbons and hydrogens show much lower Fukui indices (~0.01–0.06), confirming they are far less reactive than the Fe center. Visualization saved to `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/ferrocene/argo_claude-haiku-4.5/20260706-174414_ferrocene_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png`.</prose>
<parameter name="value">0.22885
